questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的研发投入？研发人员数量？
questions.answer: 2024年，路特斯科技在研发方面展现了强大的投入与实力。公司在研发方面的资金投入达到2.748亿人民币，充分体现了其对技术创新和产品开发的高度重视。同时，路特斯科技的研发团队规模为1,030人，进一步彰显了其在智能豪华出行领域持续深耕的决心。这些投入和人员配置不仅巩固了公司的技术领先地位，也为未来的产品创新奠定了坚实基础。以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

rag_info.sub_query: What is Lotus Technology's R&D investment in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first eleven months of 2024. As a top-selling British luxury brand, Lotus continued its strong momentum in the European market, which contributed nearly 40% of total deliveries for the eleven months ended November 30, 2024. rag_info.chunk_content: New York– June 24, 2024 –  Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced that Lotus Tech has entered into a convertible bond agreement with an aggregate principal amount of approximately \$110 million through a private placement from its existing strategic shareholder (the “Investment”). The investment marks the shareholder’s continuous confidence in Lotus Tech’s growth strategy and outlook. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and the closing is expected to take place in the near future. A description of the material terms and conditions of the convertible purchase agreement and the notes is included in the Company’s current report on Form 6-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The Investment underscores the shareholder’s strong belief in Lotus Technology Inc.’s vision and core value. The expected funds from the Investment will further strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s cash position. “Lotus Technology Inc. is grateful to its strategic shareholder as the shareholder continues to invest and demonstrate confidence in Lotus Tech’s performance and growth potential,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Tech. “Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to further executing its strategy, accelerating its growth, and importantly, furthering its mission to steer the industry towards a more sustainable future.”

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 3,180 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 56.7% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York– June 24, 2024 –  Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced that Lotus Tech has entered into a convertible bond agreement with an aggregate principal amount of approximately \$110 million through a private placement from its existing strategic shareholder (the “Investment”). The investment marks the shareholder’s continuous confidence in Lotus Tech’s growth strategy and outlook. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and the closing is expected to take place in the near future. A description of the material terms and conditions of the convertible purchase agreement and the notes is included in the Company’s current report on Form 6-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The Investment underscores the shareholder’s strong belief in Lotus Technology Inc.’s vision and core value. The expected funds from the Investment will further strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s cash position. “Lotus Technology Inc. is grateful to its strategic shareholder as the shareholder continues to invest and demonstrate confidence in Lotus Tech’s performance and growth potential,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Tech. “Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to further executing its strategy, accelerating its growth, and importantly, furthering its mission to steer the industry towards a more sustainable future.”

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the first half of 2024, technology-related revenue began to scale as Lotus Technology started to provide intelligent driving solutions and R&D services to multiple leading automotive brands. As the fleet of models equipped with state-of-the-art hardware continues to expand, paid software subscriptions represent a potential growth driver in the future. The Chapman Bespoke service, which provides customer personalization, design customizations, limited collection editions, and one-off models, was also launched to cater to growing demand. Deliveries of bespoke models are expected to further boost Lotus Technology's luxury positioning and contribute to the company's long-term premium gross margin. After assessment of the evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the United States and European Union, Lotus Technology has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company reported a total of 4,873 vehicle deliveries, reflecting a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase. This growth was well-distributed globally, with each region contributing 20-30% to the total, supported by over 200 strategically located stores. Notably, the US market accounted for 26% of total deliveries following Lotus' re-entry, indicating significant market expansion.

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans delivering 2,389 units (up 174% from 871 in 2023) and Sportscar models achieving 2,484 deliveries (a 337% increase from 568 units). The regional breakdown showed Europe leading with 1,459 vehicles (30% of total deliveries), followed by China with 1,208 units (25%), North America with 1,278 units (26%), and the Rest of the World with 928 units (19%).

In addition to strong delivery figures, the Company saw growth in technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services for major automotive brands. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service aims to cater to rising customer demand for personalization, enhancing the brand's luxury positioning and potentially boosting long-term premium gross margins.

In response to changing market conditions and uncertainties from new tariff policies in the US and EU, the Company has adjusted its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. To improve financial performance, the "Win26" plan has been initiated, focusing on achieving positive EBITDA by 2026 through process optimization, cost-saving measures, and recalibrated product strategies to better serve diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Announce Closing of Business Combination 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With both BEVs already on the roads and two more fully electric models planned for launch over the next two years, Lotus Technology Inc. is on track to become the first traditional luxury automotive brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. Since the transaction was announced, Lotus Technology Inc. has raised more than US$880 million in pre-closing and private investment in public equity (“PIPE”) financing commitments from global investors, existing shareholders, and strategic partners, representing one of the largest amounts of additional financings raised in connection with a de-SPAC transaction since 2023. “Lotus Technology Inc. is thrilled to announce its upcoming debut on the Nasdaq as it completes its business combination with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. “This is a pivotal moment in our journey. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to accelerating its growth as a listed company, leading the electric transformation of the global luxury battery electric vehicle market together with L Catterton.” “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's partnership with Lotus Technology Inc. has gotten stronger over time and our respect for the mission Lotus Technology Inc. is on has only increased,” said Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L CAA. “We are excited about the road ahead as we continue building the definitive luxury electric vehicle brand together, leveraging our global consumer insights and our strategic relationship with LVMH.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In recognition of Lotus Technology Inc.’s leadership in intelligent driving, the Company recently received approval to test Level 3 autonomous driving in Wuxi, a city near Shanghai serving as China’s pilot zone for “internet of vehicles” connectivity. This license allows Eletre drivers to use the Eletre’s Level 3 functionality on designated highways in the city. The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. As announced in January 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has entered into a definitive agreement and plan of merger (the “Merger Agreement”) with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Bar Chart Comparing Quarterly and Half-Yearly Figures  
- **Table Summary**: The chart illustrates a comparison of numerical values across two quarters (2Q23 and 2Q24) and two cumulative half-year periods (1H23 and 1H24). The data highlights a significant increase in values from 2023 to 2024 for both the quarterly and half-year periods.  
- **Context**: This visualization is part of an unaudited financial disclosure, potentially shedding light on performance trends for Lotus Technology Inc., presented in thousands. It is described within the context of forward-looking statements that include risks and uncertainties.  
- **Special Notes**: All values are in thousands, and distinctions between 2023 and 2024 are conveyed using color coding (gray for 2023, yellow for 2024).  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: In the second quarter of 2023 (2Q23), the value recorded was **1,175 thousand**.  
- **Row 2**: In the second quarter of 2024 (2Q24), the value increased significantly to **2,679 thousand**, marking more than double the value of 2Q23.  
- **Row 3**: For the first half of 2023 (1H23), the cumulative value was **1,439 thousand**.  
- **Row 4**: For the first half of 2024 (1H24), the value escalated dramatically to **4,873 thousand**, over three times the value in 1H23.  

This chart demonstrates a clear upward trend in values from 2023 to 2024 across both quarters and half-yearly periods, suggesting robust growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
[Table Level]  
- **Table Title:** Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Loss  
- **Table Summary:** This table presents the financial performance of Lotus Technology Inc. for the first half (first half) and second quarter (2Q) of fiscal year 2024 (FY2024) compared to corresponding periods in FY2023. Metrics include deliveries, revenues, cost of revenues, gross profit, gross profit margin, operating loss, net loss, adjusted net loss, and adjusted EBITDA, with year-over-year (YoY) percentage changes provided.  
- **Context:** The table details unaudited financial results to analyze performance trends over time, emphasizing significant year-over-year growth and challenges across various metrics.  
- **Special Notes:** All values are in millions of US dollars, unless otherwise noted. Unit measurements like deliveries are in units, and percentages for margins and YoY changes are explicitly listed. Footnotes 1 and 2 reference specific calculations or adjustments.  

---

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1:** Deliveries in 1H2024 reached 4,873 units, compared to 1,439 units in 1H2023, reflecting an impressive 239% YoY increase. In 2Q2024, deliveries totaled 2,679 units, up 128% from 1,175 units in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 2:** Revenues grew significantly to $398 million in 1H2024 from $130 million in 1H2023, representing a 206% YoY increase. Revenues in 2Q2024 amounted to $225 million, a 103% increase from $111 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 3:** The cost of revenues increased to $347 million in 1H2024 from $124 million in 1H2023, a 180% YoY rise. In 2Q2024, the cost of revenues was $204 million, showing a 95% increase compared to $105 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 4:** Gross profit in 1H2024 improved to $51 million, up substantially from $6 million in 1H2023, marking a 731% YoY growth. In 2Q2024, gross profit was $21 million, a 246% increase from $6 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 5:** The gross profit margin for 1H2024 stood at 13%, significantly higher than the 5% recorded in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the margin reached 9%, compared to 5% in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 6:** Operating loss expanded to $438 million in 1H2024, a 27% increase from $344 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the operating loss was $204 million, compared to $183 million in 2Q2023, marking a 12% YoY growth.  
- **Row 7:** Net loss for 1H2024 reached $460 million, a 30% increase from $353 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, net loss stood at $202 million, 4% higher than $193 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 8:** Adjusted net loss in 1H2024 was $424 million, rising 20% from $353 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, adjusted net loss was $201 million, compared to $193 million in 2Q2023, a 4% increase.  
- **Row 9:** Adjusted EBITDA in 1H2024 stood at a loss of $382 million, up 15% from $333 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, it was a loss of $177 million, almost unchanged (a 1% decrease) from $178 million in 2Q2023.

[Table Level]
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - February 23, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, rang today’s opening bell at the Nasdaq stock exchange in New York City in celebration of its public listing. The Company’s American depositary shares (“ADSs”) commenced trading today on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” following the completion of its business combination on February 22, 2024, with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”), a special purpose acquisition company (“SPAC”) formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm. Lotus Technology Inc. CEO Feng Qingfeng was joined by members of the Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton teams in New York City to witness this important milestone, which will help Lotus Technology Inc. to further its mission of becoming an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. “This is an exciting moment for all of us at Lotus Technology Inc.,” said Mr. Feng. “I would like to extend my appreciation to our shareholders, partners, suppliers, our outstanding employees, and most importantly, our customers, for their support.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is proud to carry Lotus’s heritage into the future and to open a new chapter in the Lotus story with our public listing today.” Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp believes that Lotus Technology Inc. is ideally positioned to seize the tremendous opportunity in the fast-growing and underserved luxury electric vehicle market, leveraging our global consumer insights and strategic relationship with LVMH. Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand with Racing Heritage  
- **Table Summary:** This table represents a chronological history of significant milestones and achievements by Lotus in motorsports and automotive design. Key events include early wins in Le Mans, Formula 1, and Indy 500, as well as recent industry recognitions and awards. The table highlights both competitive and design excellence over multiple decades.  
- **Context:** Lotus positions itself as a legacy brand with a rich automotive and racing history, as evidenced by numerous motorsport championships and innovative products.  
- **Special Notes:** Achievements are tied to specific years and vehicle types. The vehicle names (such as Type 18 and Lotus Evija) and awards (e.g., FIA World Champions, Luxury Briefing Awards) are integral to understanding the brand's legacy.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1 (1957):** The Lotus Eleven achieved its first victory at Le Mans in the 750cc Index Class.  
- **Row 2 (1960):** The Lotus Type 18 secured its first Grand Prix win at Monte Carlo.  
- **Row 3 (1963):** The Lotus Type 25 earned Lotus its first Formula 1 Constructors' Championship as well as the first Formula 1 Drivers' Championship.  
- **Row 4 (1965):** The Lotus Type 38 marked Lotus's first Indy 500 victory.  
- **Row 5 (1978):** The Lotus Sunbeam celebrated its first World Rally Constructors' Championship.  
- **Row 6 (2019):** Lotus was awarded the title "Luxury brand of the year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards.  
- **Row 7 (2020):** The Lotus Evija won the 2020 MUSE Global Design Awards.  
- **Row 8 (2023):** The Lotus Eletre was recognized as the 2023 GQ SUV of the Year.  
- **Row 9 (2024):** Carwow named Lotus the "Brand of the Year," bolstering its automotive excellence reputation.  

[Achievements Summary]  
- **FIA Formula 1 Constructors’ World Champions:** 7 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Drivers’ World Championships:** 6 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins:** 81 victories.  
- **Le Mans Wins (In Class):** 9 victories.  
- **Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win:** 1 victory.  
- **FIA World Rally Championships:** 1 title.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

- Table Title: Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Loss for Lotus Technology Inc.
- Table Summary: The table presents the financial and operational performance of Lotus Technology Inc. for the first half and second quarter of 2024, compared to the previous year. It includes metrics such as deliveries, revenues, costs, and profits with year-over-year percentage changes.
- Context: This data is part of a press release containing forward-looking statements by Lotus Technology Inc., which discusses potential risks and uncertainties. The table outlines part of the financial results being covered.
- Special Notes: All amounts are in US$ millions unless specified otherwise, and the figures include footnotes denoting that "Deliveries" and "Adjusted Net Loss" contain additional specifications.

[Row Level]
Row 1: In 1H2024, Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 4,873 units, a significant increase of 239% compared to 1,439 units in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, deliveries were 2,679 units, marking a 128% growth from 1,175 units in 2Q2023.
Row 2: Lotus Technology Inc. achieved revenues of $398 million in 1H2024, a 206% rise from $130 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, revenues were $225 million, representing a 103% increase over the $111 million recorded in 2Q2023.
Row 3: Cost of revenues for 1H2024 stood at $347 million, up 180% from $124 million in 1H2023, while 2Q2024 saw costs of $204 million compared to $105 million in 2Q2023, a 95% increase.
Row 4: Gross profit reached $51 million in 1H2024, showing a dramatic 731% increase from $6 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, the gross profit was $21 million, reflecting a 246% rise from $6 million in 2Q2023.
Row 5: The gross profit margin in 1H2024 was 13%, up from 5% in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, the margin was 9%, compared to 5% in 2Q2023.
Row 6: Operating loss was $438 million for 1H2024, which was an increase of 27% from $344 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the operating loss was $204 million, compared to $183 million in 2Q2023, marking a 12% increase.
Row 7: The net loss in 1H2024 was $460 million, a 30% increase from $353 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, the net loss was $202 million, slightly more than $193 million in 2Q2023, representing a 4% increase.
Row 8: Adjusted net loss was reported as $424 million in 1H2024, up 20% from $353 million in 1H2023. The 2Q2024 adjusted net loss was $201 million, compared to $193 million in 2Q2023, a 4% rise.
Row 9: Adjusted EBITDA for 1H2024 was a loss of $382 million, increasing by 15% from $333 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $177 million, slightly improving by 1% over $178 million in 2Q2023.

- Table Title: Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Loss

It includes revenue, operating expenses, net loss figures, and other comprehensive income/loss components, offering insights into Lotus Technology Inc.’s financial health during the periods.
- Context: The table is part of Lotus Technology Inc.'s earnings report and is preceded by disclaimers about "forward-looking" statements and associated risks under U.S. securities law. All amounts are expressed in thousands, except for share and per share/ADS data.
- Special Notes: The table highlights that all amounts are in thousands, share-related data is adjusted for a merger transaction in February 2024, and there are several financial metrics with specific formulas or classifications.

[Row Level]
Row 1: For the six months ended June 30, 2024, revenue generated by Lotus Technology Inc. was $398,115 thousand, compared to $130,035 thousand for the same period in 2023. This marks a significant increase in earnings.

Row 2: The cost of revenues amounted to $347,203 thousand as of June 30, 2024, rising steeply from $123,908 thousand in June 30, 2023.

Row 3: Gross profit for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was recorded at $50,912 thousand, showing a notable improvement from $6,127 thousand in the same period of the prior year.

Row 4: Research and development expenses totaled $174,854 thousand as of June 30, 2024, up from $152,548 thousand in June 30, 2023.

Row 5: Selling and marketing expenses were listed at $204,274 thousand for the six-month period ending June 30, 2024, an increase compared to $118,236 thousand for the same period in 2023.

Row 6: General and administrative expenses rose to $111,978 thousand for the six months ended June 30, 2024, from $80,417 thousand in the same period of 2023.

Row 7: Government grants provided a positive offset of $2,488 thousand in June 30, 2024, compared to $662 thousand in June 30, 2023.

Row 8: Total operating expenses reached $488,618 thousand as of June 30, 2024, compared to $350,539 thousand at the end of June 30, 2023.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

title: 6. What is the reason for the company's sales forecast adjustment?
summary: The company has made a significant adjustment to its sales forecast for 2024, revising its delivery estimate to 12,000 units. This decision stems from a cautious evaluation of the current global macroeconomic environment, which is characterized by uncertainties related to tariffs and other regulatory factors that could impact market conditions. The adjustment reflects the company's strategic response to these challenges, aiming to align its operations with the evolving economic landscape. By taking these factors into account, the company seeks to mitigate risks and ensure more accurate forecasting in a volatile market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 30. SUBSEQUENT EVENTS 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Subsequent Events**

The chapter on Subsequent Events outlines several significant developments impacting the Company following the reporting period ending December 31, 2023. 

1. **Merger Transaction:** A key event was the completion of a merger transaction on February 22, 2024, as detailed in note 1(b). This strategic move is expected to enhance the Company's market position and operational capabilities.

2. **PIPE Investments:** On February 15, 2024, the Group entered into a new PIPE (Private Investment in Public Equity) investment agreement with an investor, as noted in note 13. This investment is anticipated to provide additional capital to support the Company's growth initiatives.

3. **Share Options Granted:** Between January 1, 2024, and March 31, 2024, the Company granted a total of 5,972,760 share options to employees under the 2022 Share Incentive Plan. The options have a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per option, aligning employee interests with the Company's performance.

4. **Loans to Related Party:** On March 11, 2024, the Company’s subsidiary entered into a loan agreement with a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder. This agreement involves providing unsecured loans totaling GBP 275,000 (approximately US$354,000) for a term of 5 years at an annual interest rate of 6.95%. This transaction raises considerations regarding related party transactions and potential conflicts of interest.

5. **Termination of Pledge:** In April 2024, the Company terminated the pledge related to the land use right valued at US$32,653 and corporate buildings under construction valued at US$37,287. These assets were previously included in the operating lease right-of-use assets and property, equipment, and software as of December 31, 2023. The termination of this pledge may affect the Company’s asset management and financial leverage.

This summary encapsulates the critical subsequent events that may influence the Company's financial position and operational strategy moving forward.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 30.   SUBSEQUENT EVENTS 
summary: A merger transaction involves the combination of two or more companies into a single entity, aimed at achieving goals such as increased market share, operational efficiencies, and market expansion. This process can take various forms, including mergers of equals and acquisitions, and requires careful consideration of due diligence, valuation, regulatory approvals, and integration planning. Successful mergers can enhance competitiveness and financial performance, while failures may lead to cultural and operational challenges.

In addition to mergers, companies may utilize PIPE (Private Investment in Public Equity) investments to raise capital quickly. These investments allow private investors to purchase equity in publicly traded firms, often at discounted prices, facilitating rapid access to funds for growth or debt reduction. PIPE transactions are characterized by their speed, regulatory efficiency, and potential market impact, appealing to institutional and accredited investors.

Furthermore, companies often grant share options to employees as part of incentive programs, which can motivate staff and align their interests with shareholders. For instance, from January to March 2024, a company awarded nearly 6 million share options under its incentive plan.

Loans to related parties, which involve financial transactions between closely connected entities, are another aspect of corporate finance. Such loans require careful management to ensure compliance with disclosure and regulatory standards, as well as to mitigate associated risks. A recent example includes a subsidiary providing an unsecured loan of GBP 275,000 to a related party, with a five-year term and a 6.95% interest rate.

Lastly, the termination of pledges, such as the release of pledged assets, occurs when obligations are fulfilled or through mutual agreement. A recent termination involved land use rights and corporate buildings, highlighting the importance of formal documentation to protect the interests of all parties involved.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The application of these proposed Treasury Regulations to LCAA Public Warrants is unclear and may require gain recognition on the exchange of LCAA Public Warrants for LTC Warrants pursuant to the Merger Agreement, especially since no Qualified Electing Fund (QEF) election with respect to the LCAA Public Warrants may be made under current law. Any such recognized gain would be subject to the Default Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC) Regime (discussed below). However, it is difficult to predict whether, in what form, and with what effective date, final Treasury Regulations under Section 1291(f) of the Internal Revenue Code and any such other Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC) rules may be adopted and how any such Treasury Regulations would apply. U.S. Holders of LCAA Securities should consult their tax advisors regarding the application of Section 1291(f) of the Internal Revenue Code and the proposed Treasury Regulations promulgated thereunder in light of their particular circumstances.

metadata.title_summary:
title: PFIC Considerations of the Mergers 
summary: The summary discusses the tax implications for U.S. Holders of LCAA Securities in light of the Mergers, which may be classified as a reorganization. Despite this classification, U.S. Holders may still need to recognize gains for federal income tax purposes due to LCAA's status as a Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC). Under Section 1291(f) of the Internal Revenue Code, individuals transferring stock of a PFIC must acknowledge any gains from such transfers, irrespective of other tax provisions. Although final regulations under Section 1291(f) have not been issued, proposed regulations with a retroactive effective date suggest that U.S. Holders of LCAA Class A Ordinary Shares will need to recognize gains from the Mergers. Furthermore, U.S. Holders did not make a timely election to treat LCAA as a "qualified electing fund," which complicates their tax situation. The proposed regulations regarding LCAA Public Warrants are unclear, particularly regarding gain recognition when exchanging these for LTC Warrants. Additionally, the specifics of the final Treasury Regulations under Section 1291(f) and other PFIC rules remain uncertain, prompting U.S. Holders to seek advice from tax professionals to navigate these complexities and understand their implications.

title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Technology Inc. Financials

**Overview**: This summary provides an integrated view of Lotus Technology Inc.'s financial performance and position as of June 30, 2024, highlighting key metrics from the Parent Company Condensed Statements of Comprehensive Loss, cash flow activities, and significant equity movements following recent merger and recapitalization activities with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp.

#### Financial Position as of June 30, 2024

- **Total Assets**: Increased significantly to $2,506,254 from $1,584,678 as of December 31, 2023.
  - **Total Current Assets**: Rose to $1,279,213 from $883,712.
  - **Total Non-Current Assets**: Increased to $1,227,041 from $700,966.
  
- **Total Liabilities**: Grew to $2,680,335 from $2,411,850.
  - **Total Current Liabilities**: Increased to $1,840,454 from $1,757,281.
  
- **Shareholders' Deficit**: Improved to $(174,081) from $(1,210,702).

#### Financial Performance for the Period January 1, 2024 - June 30, 2024

- **Total Revenues**: Rose significantly to $398,115 in 2024, compared to $130,035 in 2023.
- **Net Loss**: Reported at $(460,252) for 2024, an increase from $(352,942) in 2023.
- **Loss per Ordinary Share**: Remained stable at $0.75 for 2024, compared to $0.74 for 2023.
  
- **Total Operating Expenses**: Increased to $488,618 from $350,539, driven by:
  - **Research and Development Expenses**: Increased to $174,854 from $152,548.
  - **Selling and Marketing Expenses**: Increased to $204,274 from $118,236.

- **Total Comprehensive Loss**: Reported at $(460,241) for 2024, compared to $(319,938) for 2023, with $(459,308) attributable to ordinary shareholders.

#### Key Financial Metrics

- **Cash**: Decreased to $268,781 from $418,941.
- **Restricted Cash**: Increased dramatically to $373,887 from $7,873.
- **Inventories**: Increased to $385,200 from $265,190.
- **Accounts Payable to Related Parties**: Increased to $413,779 from $340,419.
- **Convertible Notes to Third Parties**: Decreased to $75,970 from $81,635.

#### Equity Movements

As of January 1, 2024:
- **Ordinary Shares**: 474,621,603 with a value of $21.
- **Total Shareholders’ Equity (Deficit)**: $(1,205,298).

During the period:
- **Net Loss**: $(459,319), reducing equity attributable to shareholders.
- **Comprehensive Loss**: Totaled $(460,241), with $(459,308) attributable to shareholders.
  
Significant equity changes included:
- Conversion of convertible notes and preferred shares, resulting in the issuance of millions of ordinary shares and substantial increases in additional paid-in capital.
- As of June 30, 2024, ordinary shares totaled 677,231,538 with a value of $7, and additional paid-in capital reached $1,855,063.

#### Cash Flow Activities (January 1, 2024 - June 30, 2024)

- **Operating Activities**: Net cash usage was $(468,361) in 2024, compared to $(303,700) in 2023.
- **Investing Activities**: Cash used for asset purchases was $(49,510) in 2024, down from $(113,207) in 2023, with proceeds from asset disposals at $7,387.
- **Financing Activities**: Net cash inflows from financing activities were $1,248,800 in 2024, significantly up from $415,283 in 2023, driven by proceeds from the issuance of ordinary shares.

- **Cash Position**: Cash and restricted cash at the end of the period totaled $643,815 in 2024 compared to $729,027 in 2023, reflecting a net increase of $216,680 in 2024.

#### Regulatory Notes

The financial statements were prepared using consistent accounting policies, with amounts presented in thousands of US dollars. Share details are based on a par value of US$0.00001 per share, and redemption values for convertible preferred shares are included.

This comprehensive summary encapsulates the key financial metrics, performance, and significant changes in equity and cash flow activities for Lotus Technology Inc., reflecting its ongoing challenges and growth potential as of mid-2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Worldwide Research and Development Footprint**

Lotus has established a robust global Research and Development (R&D) team dedicated to driving innovation across key technological domains, with a particular emphasis on electric vehicle (EV) technologies. As of June 30, 2024, this team consists of 1,631 professionals who possess expertise in automotive engineering, software development, and artificial intelligence (AI). Their collective skills ensure that Lotus maintains strong capabilities in technology development, especially in areas related to intelligence and digitalization.

The company is actively advancing its 800-volt Electric Power Architecture (EPA) and associated technologies to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles in the luxury automotive market. In addition to improving its own product offerings, Lotus aims to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual property and software solutions to other luxury automotive brands, thereby expanding its revenue streams.

In a strategic move to bolster its technological prowess, Lotus has established Lotus Robotics in China, which functions as the company's in-house division for intelligent driving technologies. Since its inception in 2024, Lotus Robotics has been delivering comprehensive intelligent driving solutions to major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. This positions Lotus as a leading provider in the global market for intelligent driving solutions, further solidifying its commitment to innovation and technological advancement in the automotive sector.

Overall, Lotus's R&D initiatives reflect a strategic alignment with the evolving demands of the automotive industry, particularly in the realms of electric mobility and autonomous driving technologies, supported by a well-structured and highly skilled team.
